share_days: In case of holidays, delivery days, packing days etc can be changed here.
currentstock: Negative calculated quantities are still shown here, even though that of course makes no sense in practice; it can however be a helpful piece of information for review.
amount_share_type_variations: Here you can see the shares that actually need to be delivered, without jokers.
planning_long_term_harvest_shares: The data entered here is carried over into the box planning of the respective week - but only into the future. (The past is ignored.) So if you enter a vegetable here for weeks 1-20, but the current week is 10, then entries only appear in the box planning from week 11-20. The entries can simply be overwritten/deleted in the actual weekly box planning if they don't fit after all. This can also be a way, for example, to always have a head of lettuce already preset in the box planning throughout the summer months.
planning_long_term_harvest_shares_fruits: The data entered here is carried over into the box planning of the respective week - but only into the future. (The past is ignored.) So if you enter a vegetable here for weeks 1-20, but the current week is 10, then entries only appear in the box planning from week 11-20. The entries can simply be overwritten/deleted in the actual weekly box planning if they don't fit after all. This can also be a way, for example, to always have a head of lettuce already preset in the box planning throughout the summer months.
offers: Once an offer has already been ordered from, only the available packing units can still be changed. Bold & green = exists in the harvest forecast.
orders: Offers with 0 packing units of available quantity are still visible, since the quantity can also still be adjusted later under --> Offers. This keeps it visible which types of vegetables are generally available.
members: Approved members, or members who have already taken out a subscription, can no longer be deleted.
simple_planning_additional_share_types: This share type does not need weekly planning. As soon as simple planning is available, you set the constant composition here once - it then applies across all weeks.
share_weights: Here up to four weights can be entered, for the statistics.
waiting_list: If the chosen pickup day of a delivery station is full, a new subscription automatically becomes a waiting-list entry: it holds no capacity and creates no deliveries or charges. When a spot frees up, the office promotes the entry via the normal confirm action - capacity is re-checked there, and the confirm is refused while the day is still full. The position is per station and pickup day (first come, first served) and is informational only - entries can be confirmed in any order.
upload_account_data: When a month is selected, the accounts that have 0 values in H and S for this month are filtered out.
list_storages: At the moment only two storages are possible: a short-term storage and a long-term storage. The labels can however be customized.
list_offer_groups: Here offer groups can be created, which can then be assigned to the individual resellers.
delivery_exceptions: A delivery pause suspends whole weeks for a share type variation: nothing is delivered, no demand is produced and nothing is billed for that period. Already-confirmed subscriptions are adjusted automatically (future weeks only).
station_fees: Per distribution station with a configured fee, shows what the solawi owes it for the chosen period (net). Each station uses exactly one fee: per delivered box, per month, or per year. For monthly/yearly fees, pick a matching period.
list_plots: This list can then be used in the harvest forecast, so that the harvest team knows where which vegetable is to be harvested.
list_employees: These staff members are available in the weekly plan and the duty Saturdays. Inactive members stay in existing plans but can't be scheduled anew.
list_weekly_plan_categories: Categories group the weekly plan into rows. Max. rows limits how many entries a category can hold per day.
list_absence_categories: Absence categories are kept per year and used to mark absences in the weekly plan.
list_share_articles: All data here is only stored as a default. It can be changed specifically at any time in all other places such as harvest forecast, box planning etc.
logging_storage: On this page only the data of the last two weeks is shown, in order to keep the overview.
coop_shares: Negative amounts have to be paid out by the cooperative. (The member has cancelled.)
member_loans: Loans that members grant to the cooperative. Negative amounts have to be repaid to the member (e.g. after cancellation).
abos: A subscription can only be deleted or changed (except for the fields "Pickup name" and "cancelled as of") if the deliveries have not yet started. If a share is approved and the associated member has not yet been approved, the member is automatically approved as well, so that the situation does not arise where the member has not been accepted but has a share.
delivery_stations_details: In the pickup lists a pseudonym is used if the member has specified one.
default_share_articles_in_share: Each cell holds the default quantity (in the share article's default movement unit) used when planning a share. An empty cell means the article is not used in that variation. 0 or empty removes the entry.
charges_overview: Overview of the created and executed payments per member and billing run.
configuration_consents: Here the consent texts are configured that members must accept during registration.
configuration_time_management: Here delivery days, ordering days and other time-based processes are configured.
packing_list_bulk: Bulk view of the commissioning list for several box types / delivery days.
abos_emails: Send emails to subscription holders here — for example filtered by share variation, delivery station or period.
commissioning_lists_packing: These are the quantities that must be provided to the packing stations. The additional % stored on the articles (for waste, etc.) is added on top here.
payment_resellers: Only invoices that have already been finalized are shown.
